mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that sustains corporate earnings and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins and cause immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal loses predictive power when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% signals extreme monetary accommodation that compresses risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, or the signal's Sharpe ratio turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive quarterly S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates weak household demand despite macro growth, leading to earnings disappointments and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with directional accuracy below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-shock recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization that boosts short-term equity valuations and generates positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration does not precede positive 5-day returns in recovery regimes, or the mechanism yields negative alpha when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark.
